title: Cognitive decline review

examination.text: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.
examination.rationale: The exact dementia assessment section supports cognitive, behavioural and psychological history, collateral history, daily-life impact, clinician-performed physical or neurological assessment, validated tools and reviewed investigations without diagnosing dementia.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.

source.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers

treatment_and_management.text: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact monitoring section supports review of emerging needs, coordination, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

source.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans

source_limitations: Acute versus chronic course, specific cognitive domains, capacity, collateral reliability, diagnosis, investigation, and management remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
